codeReview.summary: The feature successfully implements the welcome screen and create-worktree dialog shell as specified. The implementation covers all four validation requirements (VAL-CREATE-001 through VAL-CREATE-004): zero-worktree welcome screen with creation shortcut, Ctrl+N dialog opening with focused title input, real-time derived branch/path preview, and validation blocking invalid submissions. The code follows the established patterns in the codebase with proper separation of concerns between presentation components and business logic.

issues.description: The validation regex /^\w[\w\s-]*$/ rejects titles starting with a space but allows titles starting with numbers. While this is documented behavior, it might be worth clarifying in the error message that titles must start with a letter or underscore.

The deriveSiblingPath function uses substring/lastIndexOf for path manipulation which could produce incorrect results on Windows paths with backslashes or paths without slashes. Consider using a cross-platform path library or the standard path module.
WelcomeScreen accepts onCreateWorktree prop but doesn't use it - the actual Ctrl+N handling is done in App.tsx. This prop appears to be for future use but currently creates a minor dead code path.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The codebase follows a consistent pattern of colocating tests with implementation files (*.test.tsx). The worker correctly followed this pattern for WelcomeScreen.test.tsx and CreateWorktreeDialog.test.tsx.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: All new test files are placed adjacent to their corresponding implementation files in src/components/

sharedStateObservations.observation: The theme.ts file was extended with new color aliases (fgBright, fgNormal, bgInput) to support the new components. This is a clean approach for component-specific color needs.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/app/theme.ts lines 58-61: new color aliases added consistently with existing theme structure

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker followed the worktree-tui-worker skill procedure correctly: read mission context first, wrote tests before implementation, preserved architectural separation, and ran all validators before completion.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript shows reading mission.md, AGENTS.md, and library files before implementation. Tests were created before component implementations. Both bun test and bunx tsc --noEmit were run successfully.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The CreateWorktreeDialog component demonstrates how to build a keyboard-driven dialog with OpenTUI: focused input, real-time preview updates, and Escape/Enter key handling integrated with App.tsx keyboard shortcuts.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/components/CreateWorktreeDialog.tsx shows pattern for dialog with input focused={true}, onChange handlers, and validation. App.tsx shows how to wire Escape key to close dialog while letting Enter submit.

summary: This feature implementation is complete and correct. All 4 validation requirements are fulfilled (VAL-CREATE-001 through VAL-CREATE-004). The code quality is good with proper TypeScript types, component separation, and test coverage. The worker added 6 new tests across 3 test files (WelcomeScreen.test.tsx, CreateWorktreeDialog.test.tsx, updated App.test.tsx), and all 155 tests in the suite pass. Minor non-blocking issues were identified around path manipulation edge cases and unused props but they don't impact the feature's correctness.
